proposed_hypotheses.text: Among continuous (float) features, at least one feature has a strong linear association with pfs_months. In particular, feature_078 (range 30-90, mean ~65) is positively correlated with pfs_months.
feature_013 and feature_006 are negatively correlated with pfs_months.
feature_009 is positively correlated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; stats.pearsonr(df['feature_078'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = +0.8555 between feature_078 and pfs_months (p ~ 0). Mean feature_078 by pfs quintile rises monotonically from 53.0 (Q1) to 77.0 (Q5). feature_078 is by far the strongest single predictor of pfs_months.

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr(df['feature_013'], df['pfs_months']); stats.pearsonr(df['feature_006'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_013: Pearson r = -0.153 (p ~ 3.5e-260). feature_006: Pearson r = -0.143 (p ~ 9.9e-226). Both are robustly negatively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr(df['feature_009'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_009: Pearson r = +0.123 (p ~ 1.5e-166). Higher feature_009 associated with longer pfs_months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The ordinal feature feature_057 (values 0/1/2) is monotonically associated with pfs_months: each one-level increase in feature_057 reduces mean pfs_months.

Other ordinal/multi-level integer features (feature_067 range 6-10, feature_016, feature_026, feature_033, feature_064, feature_071, feature_096) have weak or no monotonic association with pfs_months.

analyses.code: stats.spearmanr(df['feature_057'], df['pfs_months']); stats.linregress(df['feature_057'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho = -0.386 (p ~ 0); linear regression slope = -1.161 months per one-unit increase in feature_057 (p ~ 0). Mean pfs_months: 4.68 (feature_057=0), 3.49 (feature_057=1), 2.38 (feature_057=2). Strong inverse monotonic relationship — feature_057 functions as a severity / unfavorable-prognosis ordinal.

analyses.code: for c in ['feature_067','feature_016','feature_026','feature_033','feature_064','feature_071','feature_096']: stats.spearmanr(df[c], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: All other multi-level integer features are non-significant: feature_067 rho=-0.0003 p=0.95; feature_016 rho=-0.005 p=0.26; feature_026 rho=-0.003 p=0.45; feature_033 rho=+0.002 p=0.64; feature_064 rho=+0.0002 p=0.97; feature_071 rho=+0.002 p=0.72; feature_096 rho=-0.0003 p=0.95. None has detectable association with pfs_months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 77 binary features, feature_051 has a clinically meaningful negative association with pfs_months: patients with feature_051=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than those with feature_051=0.
feature_109 and feature_039 (both with prevalence ~10%) are positively associated with pfs_months — patients with these features set have longer mean pfs_months.
feature_105 and feature_043 are negatively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_051']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_051']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: 3.51 with feature_051=1 (n=27,481) vs 4.03 with feature_051=0 (n=22,519); difference = -0.521 months (Welch t-test p ~ 1.6e-181). Strong, highly significant negative effect.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind for feature_109 and feature_039
analyses.result_summary: feature_109: pfs_months 3.83 (=1) vs 3.73 (=0), diff = +0.100 mo, p=2.05e-3. feature_039: 3.83 vs 3.73, diff = +0.094 mo, p=3.29e-3. Both small but statistically significant positive effects, consistent with treatment-like benefit.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind for feature_105 and feature_043
analyses.result_summary: feature_105: diff = -0.092 mo, p=0.015. feature_043: diff = -0.048 mo, p=0.034. Both modest negative effects; significant but small in magnitude.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across categories of feature_018 (race: white, black, hispanic, asian, other).
Mean pfs_months differs across categories of feature_045 (insurance: medicare, private, medicaid, uninsured).

analyses.code: stats.f_oneway over the five race groups
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: white 3.741, black 3.741, hispanic 3.737, asian 3.797, other 3.749. One-way ANOVA F=0.564, p=0.689. No detectable marginal difference in pfs_months by race.

analyses.code: stats.f_oneway across the four insurance categories
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: medicare 3.754, private 3.736, medicaid 3.741, uninsured 3.726. ANOVA F=0.344, p=0.794. No detectable marginal difference in pfs_months by insurance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS model containing all features, feature_078, feature_057, feature_006, feature_013, feature_009, feature_092, and feature_051 are independently and significantly associated with pfs_months. feature_078 has the largest |t|.
After multivariable adjustment, race (feature_018) and insurance (feature_045) remain non-significant predictors of pfs_months.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ all 122 features (with race/insurance dummies)
analyses.result_summary: Full-feature OLS R^2 = 0.948 (adj 0.947). Top |t| coefficients: feature_078 +0.1737/unit (t=833, p~0), feature_057 -1.152/level (t=-376, p~0), feature_006 -0.0766/unit (t=-142, p~0), feature_013 -0.00271/unit (t=-116, p~0), feature_009 +0.478/unit (t=+115, p~0), feature_051 -0.375 (t=-86, p~0), feature_109 +0.154 (t=+22, p=2.2e-109), feature_039 +0.146 (t=+21, p=2.4e-100), feature_092 -0.000496/unit (t=-19, p=1.7e-82). All hypothesized predictors are independently significant.

analyses.code: Inspect race/insurance dummy coefficients in the same OLS model
analyses.result_summary: All race dummies (white, black, hispanic, asian; ref=other) have p > 0.60 (range 0.602 to 0.730). All insurance dummies (medicare, private, uninsured; ref=medicaid) have p > 0.15 (range 0.151 to 0.437). Neither feature_018 nor feature_045 is significantly associated with pfs_months after adjusting for clinical features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative association of feature_051 with pfs_months is consistent across all five race categories of feature_018 (no effect modification by race).
The positive association of feature_109 with pfs_months differs by race — feature_109 is more beneficial in some race groups than others.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_051 * C(feature_018) + clinical covariates
analyses.result_summary: Stratified raw effects of feature_051 by race: asian -0.535 mo (p=3.9e-13), black -0.506 (p=2.5e-22), hispanic -0.529 (p=8.6e-30), white -0.525 (p=3.9e-120), other -0.366 (p=4.6e-3). All negative and similar in magnitude. Adjusted feature_051 x race interaction terms all have p > 0.20 (smallest p=0.21). No evidence of race-based effect modification for feature_051.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_109 * C(feature_018) + clinical covariates
analyses.result_summary: Race-stratified raw effects of feature_109: asian +0.026 (p=0.84), black +0.253 (p=9.3e-3), hispanic +0.069 (p=0.40), white +0.088 (p=0.030), other +0.089 (p=0.72). All point estimates positive; the adjusted interaction terms feature_109 x race are non-significant (smallest p=0.59). No evidence of race-based effect modification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_051 on pfs_months is modified by feature_057 (severity ordinal): the harm from feature_051 attenuates as feature_057 rises (positive interaction coefficient).
The effect of feature_051 on pfs_months is modified by feature_078: the harm from feature_051 grows in magnitude as feature_078 rises (negative interaction coefficient).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_051 * feature_057 + clinical covariates
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient feature_051 x feature_057 = +0.0268 (p = 1.65e-5). Stratified estimates: feature_057=0 eff=-0.559 mo, feature_057=1 eff=-0.514 mo, feature_057=2 eff=-0.472 mo. Modest but significant attenuation of feature_051 harm at higher severity levels.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_051 * feature_078 + clinical covariates
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient feature_051 x feature_078 = -0.0043/unit (p = 4.55e-24). The negative effect of feature_051 grows roughly 0.04 mo more negative for every 10-unit increase in feature_078. Stratified by feature_078 quartile, raw effect of feature_051: Q1 -0.449, Q2 -0.537, Q3 -0.532, Q4 -0.538 mo. Effect amplifies particularly between Q1 and Q2.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_078 and feature_057 interact significantly: the magnitude of the feature_057 severity decrement on pfs_months grows as feature_078 increases (negative interaction).

feature_078 modifies the per-unit effects of the continuous predictors feature_006 and feature_009: the negative effect of feature_006 amplifies and the positive effect of feature_009 amplifies as feature_078 increases.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_078 * feature_057 + other covariates
analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_078 x feature_057 coefficient = -0.00951/unit (p = 3.17e-209). Extremely strong; this is the dominant interaction in the dataset. As feature_078 rises by 10, the per-level effect of feature_057 becomes roughly 0.10 mo more negative.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_078 * feature_006 + ...; OLS pfs_months ~ feature_078 * feature_009 + ...
analyses.result_summary: feature_078 x feature_006: coef = -0.00062/unit^2 (p = 1.12e-29) — negative effect of feature_006 grows with feature_078. feature_078 x feature_009: coef = +0.00436 (p = 2.55e-25) — positive effect of feature_009 grows with feature_078. Both interactions strongly significant; both directions consistent with feature_078 amplifying the effect of these continuous predictors.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_057 (severity) modifies the effects of the continuous predictors feature_006 and feature_009: at higher severity, the negative effect of feature_006 attenuates (positive interaction) and the positive effect of feature_009 attenuates (negative interaction).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_057 * feature_006 + ...; OLS pfs_months ~ feature_057 * feature_009 + ...
analyses.result_summary: feature_057 x feature_006: coef = +0.00417 (p = 2.10e-7) — feature_006 harm attenuates at higher severity. feature_057 x feature_009: coef = -0.02207 (p = 3.17e-4) — feature_009 benefit attenuates at higher severity. Both directions matched the prediction. Severity acts as a partial 'ceiling' on continuous predictor effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of feature_109 with pfs_months is consistent across insurance categories — no insurance-based effect modification.
The benefit of feature_109 is modified by feature_092: at higher feature_092 levels, the benefit of feature_109 is reduced.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_109 * C(feature_045) + clinical covariates; stratified t-tests
analyses.result_summary: Stratified raw effects of feature_109: medicare +0.148 (p=3.2e-3), medicaid +0.089 (p=0.31), private +0.054 (p=0.30), uninsured +0.100 (p=0.51). Adjusted interaction terms: medicare p=0.58, private p=0.0061 (slightly negative), uninsured p=0.28. Apart from a marginal private-insurance attenuation (p<0.01), no consistent insurance-based modification.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_109 * feature_092 + clinical covariates
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient feature_109 x feature_092 = -0.000210/unit^2 (p=0.015). Direction matches the hypothesis (benefit of feature_109 reduces as feature_092 rises) and reaches conventional significance. Magnitude is small: across the full feature_092 range (~50-830), the modulation is ~-0.16 mo.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Several lower-prevalence binary features (feature_112, feature_046, feature_108, feature_040) interact with feature_078 even though they have weak main effects.
No remaining binary feature interacts significantly with feature_057 once feature_051 is included in the model.

analyses.code: Loop OLS pfs_months ~ binary * feature_078 + covariates over all 77 binary features
analyses.result_summary: Among 77 binary features, four reach p < 0.01 for an interaction with feature_078 once feature_051 x feature_078 is also in the model: feature_112 (-0.0013, p=0.0026), feature_046 (+0.0012, p=0.0069), feature_108 (-0.0011, p=0.0090), feature_040 (-0.0017, p=0.0094). Effects are modest and would not survive strict multiplicity correction across 77 tests, but they are consistent in direction with weak modifiers of the feature_078 trajectory.

analyses.code: Loop OLS pfs_months ~ binary * feature_057 + covariates over all 77 binary features
analyses.result_summary: Aside from feature_051 itself (p=1.65e-5), no other binary feature reaches p<0.001 for interaction with feature_057. The severity interaction signal is concentrated in feature_051.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_007 is non-informative because it is constant (a single observed value) and contributes no real signal despite a numerical t-statistic.
Of the multi-level integer features beyond feature_057, none has a clinically meaningful linear trend with pfs_months once feature_078 and feature_057 are adjusted for.

analyses.code: df['feature_007'].nunique(); df['feature_007'].unique()
analyses.result_summary: feature_007 has only one unique value (0) across all 50,000 rows. The non-zero coefficient/t-statistic in the multivariable model is a numerical artifact arising from collinearity with the intercept and other constants in the design matrix. feature_007 carries no information.

analyses.code: OLS multivariable t-stats for feature_067, feature_016, feature_026, feature_033, feature_064, feature_071, feature_096
analyses.result_summary: After full adjustment, feature_067 (t=-5.4, p=5.7e-8) is the only multi-level integer that reaches even moderate significance. feature_016, feature_026, feature_033, feature_064, feature_071, feature_096 all have |t|<1.6 and p>0.10. These ordinal-looking features are essentially noise relative to pfs_months in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final synthesis: pfs_months in this prostate-cancer cohort is largely determined by feature_078 (positive linear), feature_057 (negative ordinal severity), and a modest set of continuous biomarkers (feature_006, feature_013, feature_092 negative; feature_009 positive) and one strong negative binary (feature_051). Race and insurance contribute negligibly. The clinically actionable interactions are concentrated in feature_078 x feature_057 and in feature_051's modification by feature_078 and feature_057.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_006 + feature_013 + feature_009 + feature_092 + feature_051 + feature_109 + feature_039 + feature_078:feature_057 + feature_051:feature_078 + feature_051:feature_057
analyses.result_summary: Parsimonious model containing the dominant main effects and the three strongest interactions reproduces R^2 close to the full-model 0.948. All listed coefficients are significant in the predicted direction. Race (feature_018) and insurance (feature_045) added to this model contribute no additional explained variance (delta R^2 < 0.0001) and are non-significant. Conclusion: the data are well summarized by ~7 main effects plus 2-3 interactions — cohort outcomes are not race- or insurance-modified.
